title: China-to-Indonesia Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.target_requirement: A grid-connected PV inverter must demonstrate protection behavior suitable for the Indonesian connection point, including anti-islanding, voltage and frequency trip settings, reconnection logic, and commissioning evidence requested by PLN or the relevant network operator. This is usually reviewed as part of the project grid-connection file rather than as a stand-alone import certificate.
target_standards: PLN grid-connection and commissioning review for connected distributed generation
Inverter anti-islanding, voltage, frequency, and reconnection settings requested for the project
Project-specific protection coordination and commissioning evidence
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese anti-islanding reports under GB/T 19964, GB/T 37408, NB/T 32004, or utility procedures are useful engineering evidence, but the installed firmware and settings must be confirmed against Indonesian grid parameters and project approval conditions.
rows.gap: The gap is not only whether anti-islanding exists; it is whether the exact model, firmware version, protection table, and commissioning record match the Indonesian project. Lockable settings and Indonesian-language parameter sheets reduce approval friction.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the inverter operates in parallel with PLN or another Indonesian network; evidence is project-specific and may be required before energization
mandatory_status.cn: Not transferable — China anti-islanding reports support review but do not by themselves approve Indonesian operation

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat anti-islanding as a PLN project-approval and commissioning evidence issue, not merely as a factory feature listed in the Chinese datasheet.

rows.requirement: China GB/T Baseline Versus Indonesia Market Entry
rows.target_requirement: Indonesia-facing compliance must be built around Indonesian product, trade, local-content, and grid-connection decisions. A China GB/T or NB/T technical file can demonstrate engineering maturity, but it is not an Indonesian SNI mark, ESDM rooftop PLTS approval, PLN interconnection acceptance, TKDN certificate, or K3L registration.
target_standards: Indonesian SNI, ESDM, PLN, TKDN, and K3L requirements as applicable
Product-specific Indonesian importer, project, and grid-connection files
Official Indonesian regulator or standards-body scope decisions where needed
rows.china_equivalent: The common China baseline for grid-tied PV inverters includes GB/T 37408 for photovoltaic grid-connected inverter technical requirements, GB/T 19964 for PV power-station grid connection, NB/T 32004 for grid-connected PV inverter technical specification, and voluntary CGC or CQC certification. This baseline is useful but should be mapped line-by-line to Indonesian requirements.
china_standards: CGC or CQC voluntary photovoltaic inverter certification
rows.gap: Create an Indonesia delta file rather than reusing the China dossier unchanged: SNI scope, ESDM rooftop PLTS applicability, PLN grid settings, TKDN eligibility, K3L category status, Bahasa Indonesia labels or manuals, and importer responsibilities should be explicitly tracked.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory status depends on the Indonesian gate: SNI, ESDM / PLN, TKDN, and K3L apply by product scope, project type, procurement route, and network connection
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T and NB/T evidence is commonly voluntary or project-driven for export comparison and is not an Indonesian approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Use the China GB/T file as an engineering baseline only; Indonesian market entry still requires separate SNI, ESDM / PLN, TKDN, K3L, and importer-scope checks.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation / National Public Service Platform for Standards Information

rows.requirement: K3L Scope Check for Solar Inverters
rows.target_requirement: Indonesia's Ministry of Trade operates K3L controls for safety, security, health, and environmental protection of certain goods. Solar inverters are not currently in the mandatory K3L registration list; the Permendag 21/2023 scope is mainly consumer household goods and tools rather than PV inverter equipment. Keep a written non-scope rationale for import, customer, or broker review, and reassess if the K3L list is amended or the product is bundled with a listed accessory.
target_standards: Permendag No. 21 Tahun 2023 K3L goods scope
Kementerian Perdagangan K3L goods control and registration system
Applicable K3L product-category list for imported and traded goods
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese CCC, CQC, CGC, CE, CB, or GB/T documentation may support technical review, but it is not an Indonesian Ministry of Trade K3L registration and does not by itself resolve the Indonesian distribution file.
china_standards: CCC or CQC files where applicable
rows.gap: The gap is no longer a presumed K3L registration path for the inverter. The practical task is to document why the solar inverter is outside the current mandatory K3L list, while still checking the HS code, function, voltage class, and bundled accessories against future list changes or separate listed goods.

mandatory_status.target: Not currently mandatory for solar inverters because they are not in the mandatory K3L registration list under the current Permendag 21/2023-oriented scope; keep a written non-scope rationale for import or customer review
mandatory_status.cn: Not equivalent — China product certificates do not replace Indonesian K3L registration where K3L applies

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not treat K3L registration as a current mandatory gate for solar inverters; document the non-scope position because the Permendag 21/2023 list is mainly consumer household goods and tools.

rows.requirement: ESDM Rooftop PLTS Regulation and PLN 50 Hz Grid Connection
rows.target_requirement: Indonesia rooftop solar projects connected to the network of a holder of an electricity supply business licence for public interest are governed by the ESDM rooftop PLTS regulation. Permen ESDM No. 2 Tahun 2024 removed the net-metering/export-import credit scheme: excess energy exported to PLN is now valued at Rp 0, shifting the economics toward 100% self-consumption. For projects connected to PLN, the inverter must be documented for the project application, installed capacity approval, metering and operation arrangement, and Indonesian 50 Hz grid settings used by PLN.
target_standards: Peraturan Menteri ESDM No. 2 Tahun 2024 on rooftop solar power plants connected to the electricity network of public-interest electricity supply business licence holders
PLN project-specific interconnection, metering, commissioning, and operating requirements
Indonesia 50 Hz grid-frequency settings for connected equipment
rows.china_equivalent: China grid-connection evidence under GB/T 19964, GB/T 37408, NB/T 32004, State Grid, or China Southern Power Grid procedures can support engineering review but does not substitute for ESDM rooftop PLTS compliance or PLN project approval.
rows.gap: The main gap is project approval, grid settings, and self-consumption design rather than factory safety testing alone. Because exported surplus energy is valued at Rp 0 under the current rooftop PLTS rule, exporters should prepare Indonesian-language model data, single-line diagrams, firmware setting ranges, frequency and voltage protection tables, metering compatibility, export-limiting or load-management evidence where used, and commissioning evidence for the PLN file.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for rooftop PLTS projects that connect to the covered public electricity network; PLN acceptance is project-specific, does not transfer from a China grid certificate, and export-import credit is no longer available under Permen ESDM No. 2 Tahun 2024
mandatory_status.cn: Not equivalent — China grid-code compliance does not authorize Indonesian rooftop PLTS interconnection

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat ESDM rooftop PLTS compliance and PLN 50 Hz interconnection as project approval gates separate from product-level inverter test reports; model the project for 100% self-consumption because exported excess energy is valued at Rp 0.

source.publisher: Kementerian Energi dan Sumber Daya Mineral (ESDM)

rows.requirement: SNI Marking and IEC 62109 Safety Evidence
rows.target_requirement: Indonesia uses SNI as the national standards system. For PV inverters, SNI IEC 62109 safety evidence is a relevant route for photovoltaic power-converter safety, but the mandatory legal trigger depends on whether the current Indonesian mandatory-SNI list and implementing ministry regulation cover the exact inverter category, rating, and import route. Do not apply the SNI mark or describe whole-unit certification as compulsory unless the model is confirmed in scope.
target_standards: SNI IEC 62109 series for safety of power converters for photovoltaic power systems
BSN SNI catalogue and Indonesian mandatory-SNI implementation lists
Product-specific ministry regulation where an SNI is made mandatory
rows.china_equivalent: China-market files commonly cite GB/T 37408 for photovoltaic grid-connected inverter technical requirements, NB/T 32004 for grid-connected PV inverter specifications, and IEC 62109 CB Scheme reports. These can support technical review but do not authorize Indonesia SNI marking or replace an Indonesian scope decision.
rows.gap: The gap is scope and mark control. Prepare SNI IEC 62109 test evidence and Indonesian importer documentation, but first verify the current mandatory-SNI scope before claiming that every solar inverter whole unit must carry SNI marking.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only where the current Indonesian mandatory-SNI regulation covers the exact inverter product; otherwise SNI IEC 62109 evidence may be a voluntary, tender, utility, or customer requirement
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary for Indonesian entry unless accepted as supporting evidence; China GB/T, NB/T, CQC, CGC, CE, or CB evidence does not authorize SNI marking

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat SNI IEC 62109 as the Indonesia-facing safety evidence route, but verify mandatory-SNI scope before marking the product or presenting whole-unit certification as compulsory.

rows.target_requirement: Indonesia's TKDN system records domestic-content values for products and may be required by government procurement, state-owned enterprise projects, utility tenders, incentive programs, or electricity-infrastructure rules. For PV inverter supply, a China-origin product should not be presented as TKDN-compliant unless it has a valid Indonesian TKDN assessment or is covered by a project rule that accepts the stated local-content route.
target_standards: Kementerian Perindustrian TKDN certification and product database
Project-specific procurement, electricity-infrastructure, or incentive rules that require domestic content
Valid TKDN assessment for the listed product or manufacturer where local-content claims are made
rows.china_equivalent: China-origin certificates, factory audits, bills of material, or Chinese domestic-origin declarations do not create Indonesian TKDN value. They may support a future Indonesian assessment only if the Indonesian assessor accepts the manufacturing, component, service, and cost evidence.
china_standards: Bill of materials and costed manufacturing records
rows.gap: The gap is commercial eligibility rather than electrical safety. For covered tenders or incentives, confirm the required TKDN percentage, product listing, manufacturer name, model coverage, and validity period before offering the inverter.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only for procurements, projects, incentives, or electricity-infrastructure rules that impose TKDN; not a universal product-safety certificate for every imported inverter
mandatory_status.cn: Not equivalent — Chinese origin or China domestic-content evidence does not satisfy Indonesian TKDN

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Check TKDN as a project and procurement eligibility issue; do not treat China manufacturing evidence as an Indonesian TKDN certificate.
